title: Stroke/TIA follow-up

examination.text: Supports cognition, vision, hearing, swallowing, movement, continence, communication, nutrition, strength, sensation, balance, mood and mental health, pain, activity, participation, environment, medicines, allergies, goals, discharge information, and documented follow-up planning.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped stroke residual, speech, vision, motor, function, mood, swallowing, neurological, gait, speech, power, discharge, and medication-list fields.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports cognition, vision, hearing, swallowing, movement, continence, communication, nutrition, strength, sensation, balance, mood and mental health, pain, activity, participation, environment, medicines, allergies, goals, discharge information, and documented follow-up planning.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.14 — stroke assessment and rehabilitation plan

investigations.text: Supports clinician-reviewed MRI and carotid imaging in the specified specialist-assessment context.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed brain and carotid imaging in the specialist TIA context.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed MRI and carotid imaging in the specified specialist-assessment context.

source.title: Stroke and transient ischaemic attack in over 16s: diagnosis and initial management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.4 — brain and carotid imaging after specialist TIA assessment

referral.text: Supports clinician-led secondary-prevention planning after specialist confirmation of TIA without mapping medicine names or doses.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-decided secondary-prevention documentation after confirmed TIA.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led secondary-prevention planning after specialist confirmation of TIA without mapping medicine names or doses.

source.title: Stroke and transient ischaemic attack in over 16s: diagnosis and initial management

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.1.7 — secondary prevention after confirmed TIA

follow_up.text: Supports post-transfer follow-up, information needs, daily activities, participation goals, work, driving and transport discussion, and review of health and social care needs at 6 months and annually.
follow_up.rationale: Supports mapped driving, follow-up, social participation, and longer-term review fields.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports post-transfer follow-up, information needs, daily activities, participation goals, work, driving and transport discussion, and review of health and social care needs at 6 months and annually.

source_limitations: No single exact source covers the combined stroke or TIA follow-up identity across all populations and settings.

Assessed-negative fields, blood pressure, cardiac findings, lipids, glucose, ECG, adherence, broad risk-factor or lifestyle wording, smoking, generic clinician plans, medicine-specific plan labels, fixed follow-up, and the structured history draft remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, recurrence assessment, treatment selection, urgency, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
